title: Volume with unit cubes
description: Find volume of 3-dimensional figures by counting unit cubes.
topic_path: Module 5: Addition and multiplication with volume and area

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the volume of the following figure?**

Front view | Back view
-|-
![A 3-layer figure has the following front-view arrangement, starting from left to right: 2 cubes right at the bottom layer, 1 cube back at the bottom and middle layers, 1 cube left at the bottom, middle, and top layers.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f05214d9a25ec81bce6f5ebef74f9d4df4c56bb7) | ![A 3-layer figure has the following back-view arrangement, starting from left to right: 1 cube left at the bottom and middle layers, 1 cube right at the bottom, middle, and top layers, and 1 cube back at the bottom layer.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2926345c2379f2adc8f130bac3cf1c8d5477d70e)
![A cube has a visible front, right hand side, and top faces.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d606a1905f3937b8121e6e3701d8f80554ee7bb2) is $1$ cubic unit.|

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] cubic units
hints:
**Volume** is the amount of space a solid figure takes up.

We can measure the volume of our figure by counting how many cubic units it takes to build the figure.

![A cube has a visible front, right hand side, and top faces.  A curved bracket at the bottom edge of its front face is labeled 1 unit. A  curved bracket at the left edge of its front face is labeled 1 unit. A curved bracket at the left edge of its top face is labeled 1 unit.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/97334ed47b67844890c73a9834f1020d9803d7d6) is $1$ **cubic unit** because it measures $1$ unit on every side.

The front of the figure uses $2$ cubic units.

[[☃ image 1]]

The back of the figure uses $5$ cubic units.


[[☃ image 1]]
Altogether the figure uses $2+5=7$ cubic units.
The figure has a volume of $7$ cubic units.

assessment_items.question_content:
![A cube has a visible front, right hand side, and top faces.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0157f15cfd3285822ae931b1ba7a29ad633c4507) is $1$ cubic unit.

**What is the volume of the following figure?**  
*All of the cubes are visible.*


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] cubic units
hints:
**Volume** is the amount of space a solid figure takes up.

We can measure the volume of our figure by counting how many cubic units it takes to build the figure.

![A cube has a visible front, right hand side, and top
faces. A curved bracket at the bottom edge of its
front face is labeled 1 unit. A curved bracket at the
left edge of its front face is labeled 1 unit. A curved
bracket at the left edge of its top face is labeled 1
unit.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/97334ed47b67844890c73a9834f1020d9803d7d6) is $1$ **cubic unit** because it measures $1$ unit on every side.

All of the cubes are in one layer, so we can count them.


[[☃ image 1]]
Altogether the figure uses $10$ cubic units.
The figure has a volume of $10$ cubic units.

options.alt:
A single-layer figure has the following arrangement,
starting from left to right: 3 cubes back, 1 cube
right, 2 cubes arranged in back-front direction, 2 cubes right, and 2 cubes front.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the volume of the following figure?**

Front view | Back view
-|-
![A 2-layer figure has the following front-view arrangement, starting from left to right: 1 cube back at the bottom layer, 1 cube back at the bottom and top layers, 1 cube back at the bottom layer; and from the second cube at the bottom layer, 2 cubes right.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/09d1a738c470c2a6a89b98d5da2aaedf94d70c09) | ![A 2-layer figure has the following back-view arrangement, starting from left to right: 2 cubes right at the bottom layer, 1 cube right at the bottom and top layers; from the third cube at the bottom layer, 1 cube back and 1 cube front.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/56df553ef98dff322822b324a38b66092ffbd64e)
![A cube has a visible front, right hand side, and top faces.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/92985bc15abfe4f33bbc9695867c6045fee41a55) is $1$ cubic unit. | 


[[☃ numeric-input 1]] cubic units
hints:
**Volume** is the amount of space a solid figure takes up.

We can measure the volume of our figure by counting how many cubic units it takes to build the figure.

![A cube has a visible front, right hand side, and top faces.  A curved bracket at the bottom edge of its front face is labeled 1 unit. A  curved bracket at the left edge of its front face is labeled 1 unit. A curved bracket at the left edge of its top face is labeled 1 unit.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/97334ed47b67844890c73a9834f1020d9803d7d6) is $1$ **cubic unit** because it measures $1$ unit on every side.

The bottom layer of the figure uses $5$ cubic units.

[[☃ image 1]]

The top layer of the figure uses $1$ cubic unit.


[[☃ image 1]]
Altogether the figure uses $5+1=6$ cubic units.
The figure has a volume of $6$ cubic units.

Front view | Back view
-|-
![A 3-layer figure has the following front view arrangement, starting at the bottom layer from left to right: 1 cube back at the bottom layer, 1 cube back at the bottom and middle layers, 1 cube back at the bottom, middle, and top layers, 1 cube right at the bottom and middle layers, and 1 cube right at the bottom layer.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a9817cae613b5ce89ba837ade8b328da67afe848) | ![A 3-layer figure has the following back view arrangement, starting at the bottom layer from left to right: 1 cube right at the bottom layer, 1 cube right at the bottom and middle layers, 1 cube right at the bottom, middle, and top layers, 1 cube back at the bottom and middle layers, and 1 cube back at the bottom layer.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/77b55bee1bf4676723a5ac0e7abb194855b9505e)
![A cube has a visible front, right hand side, and top faces.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e6fe77b6e01e91586ed887cfb9314f7b619d6636) is $1$ cubic unit.|

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] cubic units
hints:
**Volume** is the amount of space a solid figure takes up.

We can measure the volume of our figure by counting how many cubic units it takes to build the figure.

![A cube has a visible front, right hand side, and top faces.  A curved bracket at the bottom edge of its front face is labeled 1 unit. A curved bracket at the left edge of its front face is labeled 1 unit. A curved bracket at the left edge of its top face is labeled 1 unit.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/97334ed47b67844890c73a9834f1020d9803d7d6) is $1$ **cubic unit** because it measures $1$ unit on every side.

The back of the figure uses $6$ cubic units.


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 1]]

The front of the figure uses $1$ cubic unit.

[[☃ image 1]]
Altogether the figure uses $6+2+1=9$ cubic units.
The figure has a volume of $9$ cubic units.
